title: elephantopus micropappus
intro: Elephantopus micropappus is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae and is commonly known as the small-flowered elephantopus or small-flowered ironwort. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, including coughs and colds. Elephantopus micropappus is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are made by boiling the plant material in water or a solvent to release its active compounds.
